title: pellaea ternifolia subsp. arizonica
intro: Pellaea ternifolia subsp. arizonica, also known as the Arizona fern, is a subspecies of the Pellaea ternifolia plant, which belongs to the family Polypodiaceae and is a type of terrestrial fern native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The Arizona fern has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pellaea ternifolia subsp. arizonica include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Pellaea ternifolia subsp. arizonica can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
